agentFile.bodyIs: Structured YAML document (not markdown-with-frontmatter). The 'agent' is defined by top-level keys; natural-language behavior lives in the 'instructions' and 'prompt' string fields. Referenced via `goose run --recipe <file>` or Goose Desktop.

version.description: Recipe schema version; defaults to 1.0.0 if omitted.

title.description: Short descriptive name of the recipe.

description.description: Detailed explanation of what the recipe does.

instructions.required: conditional (at least one of instructions/prompt required)
instructions.description: Sets the agent's role/context (system-prompt-like). Parameter values are substituted before the recipe runs.

prompt.required: conditional (at least one of instructions/prompt required; required in headless/non-interactive mode)
prompt.description: Initial user prompt/message to start the session. Required when running non-interactively.

activities.description: Optional clickable example-prompt bubbles shown when the recipe opens in Goose Desktop.

extensions.description: MCP servers / extensions the recipe needs. Defines the agent's available tools. See extensions[] sub-fields.

type.allowed: stdio | builtin | platform | streamable_http | frontend | inline_python

available_tools.allowed: list of tool names; empty/omitted = all tools available
available_tools.description: Restricts which tools from the extension are exposed (tool restriction).

env_keys.description: Environment variable names needed by the extension (stdio).

bundled.description: Whether the extension ships bundled with Goose (stdio).

parameters.description: Runtime-substituted variables referenced as {{ key }} in instructions/prompt/activities. See parameters[] sub-fields.

key.description: Unique parameter name used in {{ key }} templating.

input_type.allowed: string | number | boolean | date | file | select

requirement.description: Constraint level; optional params must have a default.

default.required: required for optional params; forbidden for file params

sub_recipes.description: Sub-agents this recipe invokes; each points at another recipe file and runs as an isolated sub-agent task. See sub_recipes[] sub-fields.

values.allowed: map of parameter name to value
values.description: Pre-configured parameter values passed to the sub-recipe.

sequential_when_repeated.description: Forces sequential execution when the sub-recipe is invoked repeatedly.

max_turns.description: Maximum number of turns for subagent tasks.

retry.description: Automated retry/validation logic. Fields: max_retries (number, required), checks (array of {type:"shell", command}, required), timeout_seconds (number, default 300), on_failure_timeout_seconds (number, default 600), on_failure (string shell command).

response.description: Structured-output schema for automation workflows. Field: json_schema (object, required) — JSON schema for output validation.

bashInjection.description: Recipe instructions/prompt do not support inline shell-command injection into the prompt body (no !`cmd` substitution). Shell execution exists only as a separate structured feature in retry.checks[].command / retry.on_failure, not as in-content templating. unverified-negative (absence; no docs describe such a token).

fileInjection.description: No in-content file-include token (e.g. @file) for embedding file contents into instructions/prompt. File input is handled via parameters with input_type: file, not inline injection. unverified-negative.

argumentTokens.description: Recipe parameters declared under 'parameters' are substituted as arguments into instructions, prompt, and activities via {{ key }} mustache-style templating before the recipe runs. Values supplied via CLI/Desktop or sub_recipe 'values'. Built-in {{ recipe_dir }} resolves to the recipe directory.

toolReference.description: Tool exposure/restriction is configured structurally per extension via the available_tools array (list of tool names; empty/omitted exposes all tools). Not an in-content @tool mention token but a YAML field.

mcpReference.description: MCP servers are referenced as entries in the 'extensions' array (type: stdio | builtin | platform | streamable_http | frontend | inline_python), each declaring the MCP server/command. Structural YAML, not an in-content mention.

variableTemplating.description: Mustache-style {{ variable }} templating substitutes declared parameters and the built-in recipe_dir into instructions/prompt/activities at load time.

agentMention.description: Sub-agents are invoked by declaring them in the 'sub_recipes' array (name + path to another recipe file, with optional preset 'values'); Goose runs each sub-recipe as an isolated sub-agent. Structural declaration, not an in-content @agent mention.
